Peninsular Spanish Audios Analysis

Kaylee Fernandez

June 05, 2026

1 Design

7 participants, 90 two-syllable-noun items, each read in both conditions and in both lists. Condition a = present/paroxytone, b = past/oxytone. Everyone read list L1 first, so list = presentation order (entered centered as order). Effect direction is past − present. Decision rule: alpha = .05; for the four N-s2 cue tests, Bonferroni alpha = .0125. Contour GAMMs were inferential. Only effects reaching significance are reported below; non-significant tests are noted as null.

2 Contour models (F0, intensity)

Type. Non-parametric (GAMM; mgcv::bam, fREML, AR(1) error correction); a parametric height term plus a non-parametric difference smooth. DV. F0 (semitones re 100 Hz); intensity (dB). IVs (fixed). conditionO (ordered) carrying the height term and the s(time, by = conditionO) shape smooth; order (covariate). Random intercepts. by item. Random slopes. by-participant reference and difference factor smooths.

2.1 F0

There was a present/past difference in contour height (b = -0.22 st, t = -5.58, p < .001) and a difference in contour shape, s(time):conditionO (F(18.07, 20.12) = 5.52, p < .001). The difference was localized to the verb.

F0 contour (A) and present−past difference (B).

2.2 Intensity

There was a present/past difference in shape, s(time):conditionO (F(15.37, 18.58) = 8.63, p < .001), localized to the verb.

Intensity contour (A) and present−past difference (B). Red points mark the region where the 95% CI excludes zero.

3 N-s2 anticipation models (duration, intensity, F0 mean)

Type. Parametric (LMM; lme4::lmer, ML). Each condition effect assessed by nested model comparison, anova(restricted, inclusive) (likelihood-ratio test), reporting df, chi-square, p. DV. N-s2 duration (ms); mean intensity (dB); mean F0 (st). IVs (fixed). condition; order (covariate). Random structure. Maximal (1 + condition | participant) + (1 + condition | item_id) attempted, stepping down when singular; structure used is reported per model. Based on 7 participants, 2520 N-s2 tokens.

3.1 Duration

Random structure used: (1 + condition │ participant) + (1 │ item_id). N-s2 was shorter before an oxytone verb (b = -11.61 ms; NMC: χ²(1) = 17.20, p < .001), and remains significant after Bonferroni correction.

N-s2 duration by condition: each participant’s means (gray) and the population-level pooled estimate (black).

3.2 Intensity

Random structure used: (1 │ participant) + (1 │ item_id). No condition difference in N-s2 intensity (b = 0.06 dB; NMC: χ²(1) = 0.26, p = .611).

3.3 F0 mean (height)

Random structure used: (1 + condition │ participant) + (1 │ item_id). No condition difference in mean N-s2 F0 (b = 0.21 st; NMC: χ²(1) = 1.92, p = .166).

4 N-s2 F0 contour shape

Type. Non-parametric (GAMM; mgcv::bam, fREML). Tests whether the F0 trajectory through N-s2 differs by condition. DV. F0 (st), voiced N-s2 points. IVs (fixed). conditionO + s(t) + s(t, by = conditionO); order (covariate); t = within-syllable position. Random intercepts. by item. Random slopes. by-participant reference and difference factor smooths.

The within-syllable F0 tilt differed by condition, s(t):conditionO (F(1.00, 1.00) = 9.16, p = .002), and remains significant after Bonferroni correction.

Predicted N-s2 F0 contour by condition.

5 Summary of significant findings

Region / cue Type DV Effect (b-a) Test p
Verb F0 height GAMM F0 (st) -0.22 t = -5.58 < .001
Verb F0 shape GAMM F0 (st) tilt/shape F = 5.52 < .001
Verb intensity shape GAMM Int (dB) tilt/shape F = 8.63 < .001
N-s2 duration LMM Dur (ms) -11.61 χ²(1) = 17.20 < .001
N-s2 F0 shape GAMM F0 (st) tilt F = 9.16 = .002

The present/past stress contrast surfaces acoustically at the verb. The noun’s final syllable carries a durational anticipatory cue (shorter before oxytones). Non-significant cues are reported as null above.
